; 74F381 — 4-bit ALU, A0/B0/F0 = LSB. Combinational (no clock).
;
; Physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and a signal name is
; this file's pin name (all pins active-high here, so no '/' prefixes). ':+:'
; is the extended-dialect XOR operator (FR-079a); this part models a 74-series
; chip, so it declares no `gal:` and uses the extended dialect.
;
; Function select (S0 S1 S2), from the datasheet Function Select Table:
;   L L L  Clear   (F = 0000)
;   H L L  B minus A     (B + /A + Cn)
;   L H L  A minus B     (A + /B + Cn)
;   H H L  A plus B      (A + B + Cn)
;   L L H  A XOR B
;   H L H  A OR B
;   L H H  A AND B
;   H H H  Preset  (F = 1111)
; The two subtract modes need Cn=1 (a forced carry) to make a true 2's-complement
; difference, per the datasheet note.
;
; Each output bit is written  Fn = <half-sum SOP> :+: <carry-in SOP>  so the
; mode-selected half-sum stays a tiny sum-of-products and only the ripple carry
; grows (cf. 74283). The half-sum is Xn XOR Yn for add/xor, its complement for
; the subtracts (Xn XOR /Yn), An+Bn for OR, An*Bn for AND, 1 for preset, and 0
; for clear; the carry-in term is gated to the three arithmetic modes only, so
; it is 0 (and Fn = half-sum) in the logic/clear/preset modes. Cout is the
; bit-4 carry of the selected arithmetic operation and 0 in the non-arithmetic

; Physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and a signal name is

; chip, so it declares no `gal:` and uses the extended dialect.
;

;   L L L  Clear   (F = 0000)

;   L H L  A minus B     (A + /B + Cn)
;   H H L  A plus B      (A + B + Cn)
;   L L H  A XOR B
;   H L H  A OR B
;   L H H  A AND B
;   H H H  Preset  (F = 1111)
; The two subtract modes need Cn=1 (a forced carry) to make a true 2's-complement
; difference, per the datasheet note.
;
; Each output bit is written  Fn = <half-sum SOP> :+: <carry-in SOP>  so the
; mode-selected half-sum stays a tiny sum-of-products and only the ripple carry
; grows (cf. 74283). The half-sum is Xn XOR Yn for add/xor, its complement for
; the subtracts (Xn XOR /Yn), An+Bn for OR, An*Bn for AND, 1 for preset, and 0
; for clear; the carry-in term is gated to the three arithmetic modes only, so
; it is 0 (and Fn = half-sum) in the logic/clear/preset modes. Cout is the
; bit-4 carry of the selected arithmetic operation and 0 in the non-arithmetic

; 74245 — octal bus transceiver, 3-state (datasheet Table 3):
;   /OE=L, DIR=H : B port driven from A (A side is input)
;   /OE=L, DIR=L : A port driven from B (B side is input)
;   /OE=H        : both ports high-impedance
;
; Bidirectional pins use GALasm's tristate construct (galasmManual.txt
; section 3.4): each data pin has a .T output equation fed from the
; opposite port plus a .E enable term, and appears as a feedback input
; in the opposite equation. The pin drives only while its .E term is
; true, which makes each Bn.E/An.E pair mutually exclusive on DIR.
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator
; and signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/'

typeData.behavior:
; 7400 — quad 2-input NAND gate. Derived from the function table
; (datasheet Table 3): each output nY is LOW only when both of that gate's
; inputs are HIGH, and HIGH otherwise.
;
; This is a subunit (multi-gate) package: the four gates are independent,
; so the behavior is one sum-of-products equation per unit, each using that
; unit's own pin names. (renderas: nand fixes the drawn symbol; these
; equations are the authoritative logic.)
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (no input here is active-low). Outputs are active-high, so NAND
; is written at the output's declared polarity using De Morgan:
; nY = /nA + /nB ("nY is HIGH when nA is LOW or nB is LOW").
1Y = /1A + /1B
2Y = /2A + /2B
3Y = /3A + /3B
4Y = /4A + /4B

; 74244 — octal non-inverting 3-state buffer/line driver in two 4-bit
; groups, each group gated by its own active-low output enable. Derived
; from the function table (datasheet Table 3): with /nOE LOW each output
; nYn follows data input nAn; with /nOE HIGH that group's outputs are in
; the high-impedance OFF-state.
;
; 3-state outputs use GALasm's tristate construct (galasmManual.txt section
; 3.4, established on the 74245): each output has a .T equation fed from its
; data input plus a single-term .E enable. The pin drives only while its .E
; term is true; when /nOE is HIGH the .E term is false and the output is
; high-impedance.
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and
; signal names are this file's pin names with any active-low '/' prefix
; dropped (pin /1OE is GAL signal 1OE, so '/1OE' below means "pin /1OE is
; LOW").
1Y0.T = 1A0
1Y0.E = /1OE
1Y1.T = 1A1
1Y1.E = /1OE
1Y2.T = 1A2
1Y2.E = /1OE
